name: lruttl
description: A thread-safe, generic (Go 1.23) LRU cache with per-entry TTL expiry built on stdlib container/list, sync, and time. Combines recency-based eviction (capacity-bounded, least-recently-used dropped on overflow) with lazy time-based expiry (expired entries removed on read). API: New, Get (refreshes recency), Put (insert/update, re-arms TTL), Len. An injectable clock (the unexported now field) makes TTL behavior deterministically testable. Verified GREEN: go build, go vet, and go test (incl. -race) all pass.

maintain.request: Add a Peek(key) (V, bool) method that returns a value without affecting its LRU recency (so it cannot save an entry from eviction), and a Delete(key) bool method that removes an entry and reports whether it was present. Both must respect the existing lazy-expiry semantics.

review.bug: Len() reads c.ll.Len() without acquiring c.mu, so it races with Put/Get/Delete which mutate the list under the lock, producing an unsynchronized concurrent read (data race) on a type documented as safe for concurrent use.
keywords: len does not acquire the mutex
data race on the linked list
